contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 35
- Description: Blog: 'Three times is when you name it' (Field Signals #5)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/81
  
  Details:
  Drafted, illustrated, and shipped a meta-essay on the rule-of-three heuristic that surfaced after Gary asked whether the morning's pattern-naming arc was itself a pattern.
  
  ## What shipped (truesight_me_beta PR #81, squash-merged)
  - blog/posts/three-times-is-when-you-name-it.html — ~1,100 words, 6 H2 arcs:
    1. The rule of three (heuristic stated cleanly)
    2. Two patterns we just named (Four-Wire Loop + Build/Document/Story as worked examples, each via 3 concrete shipped instances this week)
    3. What naming costs (early naming locks in premature shape; late naming leaks institutional debt — both costs named)
    4. The third instance as evidence (why three and not 2 or 4)
    5. What changes when the name lands (reviewer checklist + shorter onboarding + nameable failure modes + operator judgment shifts up a rung)
    6. A small confession (the rule itself was a fourth-instance move; pull-quote 'Wait for the third instance. Then write.')
  - assets/blog/three-times-is-when-you-name-it.svg — three glyphs in a row, first two dim with a '?' next to the second (coincidence vs convergence), third haloed with a handwritten PATTERN label hanging off it on a string. Numeric tally above (1/2/3), italic caption below.
  - blog/index.html — Field Signals #5 card at the top, succeeding #4 (The system that broke is the system that proposes the fix).
  
  ## SVG bug caught + fixed pre-merge
  The first version had decorative dashes inside XML comments ('---------- INSTANCE 1 ----------') which silently broke strict-XML parsing in browsers (HTML is forgiving, SVG isn't). Same bug-pattern shape as the heredoc-$host bug from the Edgar migration: same character looks fine to the human, breaks on the stricter parser. Fixed before merge; documented in commit message so the next session catches the same trap faster.
  
  ## Editorial framing constraint honored
  Per my Seth-Godin-CMO assessment (and Gary's go-ahead): lead with the universal heuristic (rule of three), use our two patterns as worked examples in the body, name both costs of getting the timing wrong, close with the small confession. Avoided the announcement-shaped post ('we just wrote two design docs').
  
  PR: https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/81 (merged)
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 58.33
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
